pageDescriptionAfter: Free tenths-to-inches converter for surveying, construction, and engineering measurements.
hintDirection: Surveyors and builders often work in decimal feet (tenths); convert to inches for tape measures and specs.
hintValue: Enter decimal feet (such as 3.7 ft) or inches depending on the direction you selected.
hintPrecision: Match the precision used on your survey notes, CAD export, or field worksheet.
importantBody: This tool converts between decimal feet and inches using 12 inches per foot. It does not replace official survey plat dimensions or engineered drawings—verify critical measurements in the field.
resultsSubtitle: Based on your inputs and the standard 12 inches per foot formula
emptyHint: Enter a measurement and click “Convert measurement” to see your result here.
formulaRefNote: Standard conversion factors used by this calculator:
proTipsTitle: Pro tips for decimal feet conversions
proTip1: GPS and survey data often export elevations in decimal feet—multiply by 12 before comparing to inch-scale drawings.
proTip2: One tenth of a foot (0.1 ft) always equals exactly 1.2 inches—a handy mental check in the field.
proTip3: When reading tenths on a rod or story pole, 3.7 ft means 3 feet plus 7 tenths (not 3 feet 7 inches).
proTip4: For reverse conversion, divide inches by 12 to get decimal feet (e.g. 44.4 in ÷ 12 = 3.7 ft).
proTip5: Round only at the end of a calculation chain to avoid cumulative error on long runs.
errValue: Enter a valid number (0 or greater).
